title: Govern, map, measure and manage

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Govern, map, measure and manage; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: NIST AI RMF organizes continuous risk work through Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage: assign accountability, understand context, assess prioritized risks, apply controls, monitor, respond, and document residual risk.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A risk register names affected stakeholders, owner, scenario, likelihood/impact evidence, preventive and detective controls, residual risk, monitor, and stop criterion. Your goal is to produce a concise AI risk register with owners, evidence, controls, residual risk and stop criteria. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Supervised learning; Generative AI, LLMs and retrieval; Robustness, drift and adversarial cases; Fairness and explainability; API, tool and prompt-injection boundaries. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: NIST AI RMF organizes continuous risk work through Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage: assign accountability, understand context, assess prioritized risks, apply controls, monitor, respond, and document residual risk. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: The functions are not a one-time checklist or ordered certification, risk tolerance is contextual, measurement can be incomplete, third parties share responsibility, and controls need owners and evidence. The learner must reject this overclaim: Completing an AI RMF checklist certifies an AI system as safe and transfers responsibility to the framework.

includes: Assign ownership, document context, measure prioritized risks and implement proportionate controls and incident response.
Produce a concise AI risk register with owners, evidence, controls, residual risk and stop criteria.
excludes: The functions are not a one-time checklist or ordered certification, risk tolerance is contextual, measurement can be incomplete, third parties share responsibility, and controls need owners and evidence.

outcomes: Produce a concise AI risk register with owners, evidence, controls, residual risk and stop criteria.

Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: Completing an AI RMF checklist certifies an AI system as safe and transfers responsibility to the framework.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: Completing an AI RMF checklist certifies an AI system as safe and transfers responsibility to the framework.

analogy.example: an evidence and risk register linking metrics, stress cases, affected slices, accountable owners, controls, monitors, and stop decisions can introduce Govern, map, measure and manage only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: evaluation, robustness, fairness, explanations, and lifecycle governance
limitations: no metric suite or framework checklist certifies safety, fairness, causality, or future robustness
